description: A scalar to:"on" trigger with for: combines semantic-group expansion and duration-hold debounce the way the field incident it is modeled on required: the device repeatedly bounces off the off-group into an on-group member ("suspend") that is not the literal string "on", each brief bounce is discarded by the for: hold exactly as a literal on/off bounce would be, and only a later occurrence that holds continuously for the full duration dispatches.

events.note: "suspend" is not the literal string "on" but is a member of the screen device class's on-group (RUL-321 scalar expansion); matches to:"on" and starts the hold
reverts after 3000ms, short of the 20000ms for:; pending fire discarded
bounces back into the same on-group member; matches to:"on" again via expansion, hold restarts
reverts after 2500ms; discarded again — this is the Suspend-flap shape
settles on the literal state "on" (also an on-group member); hold restarts
on has held continuously since seq 5 (20000ms of monotonic time)

results.reason: off->suspend matches to:"on" via semantic-group expansion (RUL-321), starting the for: hold
hold broke at 3000ms elapsed, short of the 20000ms requirement
off->suspend matches to:"on" again via group expansion; hold restarts from zero
hold broke at 2500ms elapsed, short of the 20000ms requirement; two flap cycles now discarded without ever dispatching
off->on matches to:"on" by literal equality; hold restarts from zero
on held continuously from t=7000 to t=27000, satisfying for: 20; this is the only dispatch across the whole sequence
